title: ++ concat and |> pipe
manager: Concatenation (`++`) for joining sequences and strings, and the pipe operator (`|>`) for threading a value through a chain of transforms left to right. The pipe is what turns a nested pile of function calls into a readable pipeline — `x |> f |> g` instead of `g(f(x))` — which matters enormously once the expression language and stdlib give you functions actually worth chaining.
cs: `++` sequence/string concat; `|>` left-to-right application; precedence pinned in W6.38; pairs with the HOFs (W6.43).
